maml:description.maml:para: Refreshes or repairs an Azure Artifacts private PowerShell module repository registration.

This cmdlet uses the same repository resolution logic as Register-ModuleRepository, but is intended for reconnecting or repairing repository configuration after credentials, endpoints, or tool-specific registration details have changed.
The output object indicates which native install paths are ready after refresh, so callers can see whether Install-PSResource, Install-Module, or both are available for the configured repository.
Azure Artifacts feed name.
Azure DevOps organization name.
Optional Azure DevOps project name for project-scoped feeds.

Bootstrap/authentication mode. Auto uses supplied or prompted credentials when requested; otherwise it prefers ExistingSession when Azure Artifacts prerequisites are ready and falls back to CredentialPrompt when they are not.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Installs missing private-gallery prerequisites before refresh, including PSResourceGet requirements and, for Azure Artifacts, the credential provider.
JFrog Artifactory base URI, for example https://company.jfrog.io/artifactory.
JFrog NuGet repository key. Defaults from Repository when omitted.
Optional repository name override. Defaults to the feed name.
Optional PSResourceGet repository priority.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials.
Private gallery provider.
Provider repository/feed id. For Azure this is the feed when AzureArtifactsFeed is omitted; for JFrog this is the Artifactory NuGet repository key.
PowerShellGet publish URI for generic/JFrog feeds.
PowerShellGet source URI for generic/JFrog feeds.
PSResourceGet v3 repository URI for generic/JFrog feeds.
Registration strategy. Auto prefers PSResourceGet and falls back to PowerShellGet when needed.
When true, marks the repository as trusted.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Installs missing private-gallery prerequisites before refresh, including PSResourceGet requirements and, for Azure Artifacts, the credential provider.
Saved repository profile name.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials.
Installs missing private-gallery prerequisites before refresh, including PSResourceGet requirements and, for Azure Artifacts, the credential provider.
Refreshes Microsoft Artifact Registry registration for Microsoft-owned packages.
Optional repository name override. Defaults to the feed name.
Optional PSResourceGet repository priority.
Provider repository/feed id. For Azure this is the feed when AzureArtifactsFeed is omitted; for JFrog this is the Artifactory NuGet repository key.
Registration strategy. Auto prefers PSResourceGet and falls back to PowerShellGet when needed.
When true, marks the repository as trusted.
Azure Artifacts feed name.
Azure DevOps organization name.
Optional Azure DevOps project name for project-scoped feeds.

Bootstrap/authentication mode. Auto uses supplied or prompted credentials when requested; otherwise it prefers ExistingSession when Azure Artifacts prerequisites are ready and falls back to CredentialPrompt when they are not.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Installs missing private-gallery prerequisites before refresh, including PSResourceGet requirements and, for Azure Artifacts, the credential provider.
JFrog Artifactory base URI, for example https://company.jfrog.io/artifactory.
JFrog NuGet repository key. Defaults from Repository when omitted.
Refreshes Microsoft Artifact Registry registration for Microsoft-owned packages.
Optional repository name override. Defaults to the feed name.
Optional PSResourceGet repository priority.
Saved repository profile name.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials.
Private gallery provider.
Provider repository/feed id. For Azure this is the feed when AzureArtifactsFeed is omitted; for JFrog this is the Artifactory NuGet repository key.

maml:description.maml:para: Updates one or more modules from a private repository, optionally refreshing Azure Artifacts registration first.
This cmdlet is the day-to-day maintenance companion to Install-PrivateModule. When Azure Artifacts details are provided, the repository registration is refreshed before the update is attempted.
Accept package licenses when packages declare license acceptance is required.
Allow command exports to overlap with other modules in the managed target root.
Allow updating even when matching loaded module evidence is supplied to managed delivery.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Reinstall the selected target version when managed delivery is used.
Maximum target version when Version is omitted.
Minimum target version when Version is omitted.
Explicit module root used by managed delivery.
Module names to update.
Optional managed package cache directory.
Includes prerelease versions when supported by the selected installer.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials.
Name of an already registered repository, or provider repository/feed id when a private-gallery provider is selected.
Optional repository name override when repository details are supplied.
Require managed receipt source evidence to match the requested repository before reporting up to date.
Scope inspected by managed delivery, or by compatibility delivery when explicitly supplied.
PowerShell path family used when managed delivery resolves default module roots.
Skip installing dependencies declared by the package when managed delivery is used.
Delivery engine used for module update.
Exact target version. When omitted, the latest repository version is used.
NuGet-style version range policy used by the managed transport when Version is omitted.
Accept package licenses when packages declare license acceptance is required.
Allow command exports to overlap with other modules in the managed target root.
Allow updating even when matching loaded module evidence is supplied to managed delivery.
Azure Artifacts feed name.
Azure DevOps organization name.
Optional Azure DevOps project name for project-scoped feeds.

Bootstrap/authentication mode used when Azure Artifacts details are supplied. Auto prefers ExistingSession when Azure Artifacts prerequisites are ready and falls back to CredentialPrompt when they are not.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Reinstall the selected target version when managed delivery is used.
